summary: Personalized vitamin and supplement recommender. Multi-step quiz feeds a rule-based engine that produces explained recommendations, an explainable health score, drug/supplement interaction warnings, recommendation history, cost-optimized alternatives, and a shareable read-only result page. Built with Next.js 16 + Supabase, integrating a custom 'nutrition-domain' Claude Code Skill and a 'supplement-evidence' MCP server.
constraints: Stack is fixed: Next.js 16 (App Router, TS), Tailwind v4, Supabase (Postgres + magic-link auth), Vercel.

This is Next.js 16 / React 19 — newer than Claude's training cutoff. Before writing or editing any Next.js code, read the relevant guide in node_modules/next/dist/docs/ and respect the warnings in AGENTS.md.
Use the nutrition-domain Skill (.claude/skills/nutrition-domain/) whenever editing files under lib/engine/.
Use the supplement-evidence MCP server (.claude/mcp/supplement-evidence/) for citation lookups; the server runs at dev/build time only — its JSON data is bundled for runtime.
Every user-facing recommendation surface must show a medical disclaimer and respect age/pregnancy gating.
Per-user data is isolated via Supabase RLS — never bypass RLS in application code.
Use npm (not pnpm/yarn) — verification commands assume npm scripts.
Goals execute one at a time per the spec-driven workflow; flip status pending -> passed only after the goal's verification command passes.
assumptions: User has (or will create) a Supabase project and a Vercel account; they will paste env vars when goal 2 / 3 ask for them.
Curated supplement dataset of ~30 entries is sufficient for the demo (no external API integrations).
Demo audience is the AUM AI Agentic class in Week 16 of Spring 2026.
goal_selection_rule: Work the first pending goal whose dependencies are all passed.

goals.goal: Next.js 16 + TS + Tailwind scaffold with placeholder VitaPath landing page; spec.json, .claude/ skill+MCP scaffolding, and updated README all committed; live on Vercel.

checklist: git log shows initial 'chore: vitapath scaffold + spec.json' commit
https://vitamin-chi.vercel.app/ returns 200 and renders the VitaPath landing copy
spec.json present at repo root and parses as valid JSON

goals.goal: Supabase project linked to repo via env vars; supabase/migrations/0001_init.sql applied (profiles, quizzes, recommendations, share_tokens with RLS + policies; get_shared_result security-definer RPC for shareable results).

checklist: All 4 tables exist in public schema with RLS enabled
Per-table policy counts: profiles=3, quizzes=2, recommendations=2, share_tokens=2
get_shared_result(text) RPC exists and is grantable to anon

goals.goal: Magic-link auth flow: sign-in form, callback handler (PKCE code + token_hash verifyOtp), sign-out route; /history protected by proxy.ts and redirects unauthenticated users to /login.

tests_to_write: e2e: anonymous /history redirects to /login
e2e: login page renders the magic-link form
e2e: admin-minted magic link signs user in, /history shows email, sign-out re-protects /history
checklist: Anonymous visit to /history redirects to /login
Sign-out clears the Supabase session cookie (verified by post-signout redirect back to /login)

goals.goal: nutrition-domain Skill scaffolded with SKILL.md and reference JSON files (nutrient_rdas.json, interactions.json, contraindications.json) that validate against a Zod schema in lib/engine/schemas.ts.

tests_to_write: All reference JSON files parse against their Zod schemas
Skill loads without errors when listed by Claude Code

goals.goal: supplement-evidence MCP server runs over stdio and exposes get_supplement, list_studies, search_evidence tools backed by data/supplements.json; registered in .mcp.json at repo root.

checklist: All 3 tools (get_supplement, list_studies, search_evidence) advertised
get_supplement('vitamin_d') returns the seeded record
list_studies('omega_3') returns ≥1 study
search_evidence(magnesium_glycinate, 'sleep') returns ≥1 study
(manual) `npm run inspect` from .claude/mcp/supplement-evidence/ opens the official Inspector UI showing the same

goals.goal: Multi-step quiz UI at /quiz/[step]: 5 sections (basics, diet, sleep, stress, lifestyle), URL-routed steps, Zod-validated forms (per-step + merged), progress bar, draft persisted in localStorage across refresh, final-step server action creates a quizzes row.

tests_to_write: e2e: anonymous /quiz/1 redirects to /login
e2e: refresh on step 3 + back-nav preserves steps 1-2 answers
e2e: complete all 5 steps + final submit creates a quizzes row in Supabase

goals.goal: Pure recommendation engine in lib/engine/rules.ts: takes typed QuizAnswers, returns deduped Recommendation[] (supplement_slug, name, dose, merged rule_ids, rationale[], pregnancy_safe). Sources of truth are the validated nutrition-domain refs + supplements.json. Pregnancy gates the universe (only pregnancy_safe items shown).

tests_to_write: 16 unit tests: low-sun → vitamin D, vegan → B12, high-stress → Mg, poor-sleep → Mg, low-fish → omega-3, premenopausal-female → iron, pregnancy → folate + omega-3 + iron, healthy male → no recs, postmenopausal-female → no female-iron, dedupe (one Mg rec for stress+sleep), pregnancy gating invariant, sorted output, purity, dose presence, ≥10 unique rule ids

goals.goal: Explainable health score in lib/engine/score.ts: pure function returning { baseline, raw_score, score (clamped 0-100), contributions[] }. Each contribution = { rule_id, label, delta }; sum(contributions.delta) === raw_score - baseline by construction.

tests_to_write: Neutral inputs → score === baseline, no contributions
Sum invariant holds for 5 sampled inputs
Contributions sorted by |delta| desc, stable on ties via rule_id
Score clamps to [0, 100]; raw_score may exceed
Deterministic across calls
All 16 rule ids unique; every emitted contribution has non-zero delta

goals.goal: Safety / interaction checker in lib/engine/interactions.ts: pure (answers, recs) → { recommendations, warnings }. Three warning categories: pregnancy_removed (defensive re-gate), condition_removed/condition_warning (from contraindications.json), drug_interaction + supplement_interaction (from interactions.json). Medication name verbatim per skill rule 4. Sorted by severity desc.

tests_to_write: St John's Wort + SSRI → high drug_interaction with 'ssri' verbatim
Vitamin K + warfarin → high drug_interaction with 'warfarin' verbatim
Iron + calcium recs → moderate supplement_interaction (timing)
Pregnancy + non-safe rec → removed + high pregnancy_removed warning
Pregnancy + folate → kept, no warning
No relevant meds/conditions → empty warnings
Hemochromatosis + iron → condition_removed (from contraindications.json)
Multi-warning ordering: high severities before moderates
Deterministic across calls

goals.goal: Results page at /results: server component pulls latest quiz (RLS-scoped), runs score + rules + interaction checker, renders score gauge with contribution breakdown, severity-tinted warnings, and recommendation cards with evidence citations bundled from supplements.json. saveQuiz extended to compute score and persist recommendations rows.

tests_to_write: e2e: anonymous /results redirects to /login

e2e: full quiz → /results renders score in [0,100], expected rec slugs (vitamin_d, vitamin_b12, omega_3, iron for vegan-low-sun-female profile), each with ≥1 citation showing a 4-digit year, and the medical disclaimer is on the page
DB sanity: a recommendations row exists per visible card

goals.goal: /history page lists past quizzes (date, score, rec count) and renders a Recharts line chart of score over time. Each row deep-links to /results/[id] which renders the frozen result for that quiz via a shared ResultsView server component.

tests_to_write: e2e: after 2 quizzes, /history shows exactly 2 rows + the Recharts trend chart
e2e: clicking a row navigates to /results/<uuid> and the frozen view renders score + rec list

goals.goal: Cost-optimized alternative for each recommendation. supplements.json gains a brands[] field with synthetic prices and meets_dose flag. Pure chooseAlternative() picks the cheapest brand that meets the dose AND is cheaper than the primary; null otherwise. <RecCard> client component shows a toggle to swap between primary and alternative without page reload.

tests_to_write: Selector returns cheapest brand meeting dose when cheaper than primary
null when only 1 brand
null when no brand is cheaper than the primary
filters out brands that don't meet dose
returns the cheapest among multiple eligible alternatives
getPrimaryBrand falls back to first brand when no is_primary flag
getPrimaryBrand returns explicitly flagged primary even when first is cheaper
e2e: clicking toggle on /results updates brand price text without navigation

goals.goal: Shareable read-only /r/[token]. createShareToken server action mints a 32-char base64url token via crypto.randomBytes, inserts into share_tokens (RLS gates ownership, default expires_at = now + 30 days). The public /r/[token] page calls get_shared_result via an anonymous Supabase client and renders ResultsView in mode='shared' (no /history or share buttons). ShareButton client component on /results reveals a copyable URL.

tests_to_write: e2e: authed user clicks share, share-link-box appears with /r/<token>
e2e: fresh browser context (anon) visits the URL — score-section + rec-list visible, no share-button present
e2e: admin-inserted expired share_token + visit to /r/<token> shows the expired-state UI

goals.goal: Pre-quiz gating. /disclaimer page acknowledges via a server action that sets vitapath_disclaimer_v1 cookie (1y, sameSite lax). proxy.ts redirects authed users from /quiz/* to /disclaimer when the cookie is absent. Step 1 of the quiz routes age_band='13-18' to /under-18 (a blocking pediatrician page). Pregnancy is captured up-front and the engine consumes it via the existing saveQuiz pipeline.

tests_to_write: e2e: authed visit to /quiz/1 without disclaimer cookie → /disclaimer?next=/quiz/1
e2e: clicking accept on /disclaimer drops the cookie and lands on /quiz/1
e2e: selecting 13-18 on step 1 navigates to /under-18 (and clears the draft)
e2e: pregnancy=yes flows through saveQuiz; DB row's answers.pregnancy_status === 'yes' AND a folate row exists in recommendations

goals.goal: Final polish: README rewritten with setup, env vars, MCP/Skill docs, mermaid architecture diagram, full goal table, manual demo checklist. All test suites green. Production deploy live at vitamin-chi.vercel.app. Repo public on GitHub.

verification.command: npm test && npm run test:e2e && npm run build
checklist: 45/45 vitest passing
14/14 e2e passing across 6 specs
npm run build completes with zero type errors

README contains: hero, live URL, mermaid architecture diagram, env vars, install, deploy, spec-driven workflow goal table, Skill + MCP integration sections, manual demo checklist (9 items including Lighthouse), repo layout

goals.goal: Visual identity pass: opengraph-image.tsx generates a 1200x630 sage social card; explicit openGraph + twitter metadata in app/layout.tsx; landing hero gains a portrait Unsplash photo on the right (next/image with priority, hidden on mobile, sage-soft frame); /history empty state adopts a SprigIcon + serif heading + body; each RecCard prefixes the supplement name with a sage SprigIcon for editorial rhythm. No engine data or test contracts touched.

checklist: app/opengraph-image.tsx exists and renders 1200x630 with VitaPath brand mark + headline
/public/hero/landing-hero.jpg present (Unsplash, free under Unsplash License)
app/layout.tsx exports metadataBase + openGraph + twitter metadata
Landing hero shows the photo on md+ viewports, hidden on sm
History empty state renders SprigIcon + 'Your trend starts here.' heading
RecCard header shows SprigIcon left of supplement name

goals.goal: UX polish pass: Next.js loading.tsx skeletons for /results and /history that mirror the real page layout (rounded score placeholder, three placeholder rec cards, placeholder history rows) so the streaming wait feels intentional; RecCard gains a subtle hover lift (translate-y-0.5 + shadow-md + sage border) gated by motion-reduce. Reuses existing tokens (duration-fast, ease-out-soft, surface-soft).

verification.command: npm run lint && npm run build && npm test && npm run test:e2e
checklist: app/results/loading.tsx and app/history/loading.tsx exist and render shape placeholders
RecCard has hover:-translate-y-0.5 + hover:shadow-md + motion-reduce:transform-none
Loading skeletons include aria-live polite status for screen readers

goals.goal: Migration 0002_share_tokens_delete.sql adds the missing share_tokens_self_delete RLS policy. revokeShareToken in lib/share/actions.ts drops the admin-client fallback and issues the DELETE through the user-scoped client; Postgres enforces ownership via RLS. verify-db.mjs bumps the expected share_tokens policy count from 2 to 3.

verification.command: npm run verify:db && npm run lint && npm run build && npm test && npm run test:e2e
checklist: supabase/migrations/0002_share_tokens_delete.sql present with the share_tokens_self_delete policy
npm run verify:db ends with 'share_tokens: 3 RLS policies in place' and 'All Goal 2 checks passed.'
lib/share/actions.ts no longer imports createAdminClient
revokeShareToken now issues a single DELETE through the user-scoped client (RLS-enforced)
45/45 vitest passing
14/14 e2e passing — share spec still covers the mint + visit + expired flow
npm run lint clean, npm run build clean

goals.goal: Ralph Wiggum voice-cleanup loop: scripts/audit-voice.mjs mechanically scans every .tsx under app/ for v2 voice violations (em dashes in user copy, AUM AI Agentic tagline, literal `←`) after stripping JS comments. .claude/ralph/voice-cleanup.md is the autonomous-loop prompt the user pipes to /loop — Claude reads each flagged line, decides real-vs-false-positive, edits as needed, and re-iterates until exit 0. npm run audit:voice wires the script into the standard verification commands. AGENTS.md gains a Voice rules + Ralph loop section so any fresh Claude session knows the policy.

checklist: scripts/audit-voice.mjs exists and exits 0 on a clean codebase, 1 with violations listed file:line:rule
.claude/ralph/voice-cleanup.md exists with the loop prompt + invocation instructions
package.json exposes `audit:voice` script
AGENTS.md references the auditor + the Ralph loop file
`npm run audit:voice` reports `voice audit clean across N .tsx files`

goals.goal: Lighthouse audit captured for the demo: docs/LIGHTHOUSE.md records mobile scores (Perf 88, A11y 95, Best Practices 100, SEO 100), Core Web Vitals (LCP 3.0s, FCP 1.5s, TBT 50ms, CLS 0, Speed Index 6.5s, TTI 3.0s), the single flagged opportunity (Reduce unused JavaScript, 150ms), and notes about the LCP candidate on mobile.

checklist: docs/LIGHTHOUSE.md exists with all four category scores
Core Web Vitals captured (LCP, FCP, TBT, CLS, Speed Index, TTI)
Re-run command documented so the audit can be repeated

goals.goal: HANDOFF.md trimmed from 639 to ~430 lines: dropped the full commit history list, compressed the repo-layout tree to top-level only (linked to GitHub), trimmed the production deployment section to bare-essential steps, updated the spec progress table to 18 goals, added Lighthouse + Ralph loop references, and refreshed the resuming-on-a-fresh-session checklist.

checklist: docs/HANDOFF.md under 450 lines
Spec progress table lists goals 1-18 with their status
References section points to docs/LIGHTHOUSE.md
Resuming-on-fresh-session checklist mentions the Ralph loop

goals.goal: LLM 'Why this for me?' expander on each RecCard. Server action lib/explain/actions.ts re-fetches the quiz from Supabase (RLS-scoped), rebuilds the recommendation via the pure engine, calls Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile through groq-sdk, and caches the 2-sentence response in public.rec_explanations (migration 0003). RecCardExplain client component renders a disclosure under the Evidence section; quizId is threaded from ResultsView through ResultsInteractive only in mode=personal so the shared /r/[token] view stays anonymous. Voice rules baked into the system prompt.

verification.command: npm run lint && npm run build && npm test && npm run audit:voice
checklist: supabase/migrations/0003_rec_explanations.sql applied; npm run verify:db sees 5 tables + rec_explanations: 2 policies
GROQ_API_KEY present in .env.local and Vercel production
RecCard renders the expander only when quizId is provided
First click triggers server action; subsequent opens reuse the cached row from rec_explanations
45/45 vitest, lint + build + audit:voice clean

definition_of_done: All 15 goals are passed (status = 'passed' in spec.json)
Production app reachable on Vercel and demo checklist green
